      "それはエジプト人の誇りを鼓舞することで知られています。",

About: It is about Arab nationalism and Arab socialism.
Affects: It affects the domestic and international spheres.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Gamal Abdel Nasser.
It is associated with the Egyptian Revolution of 1952.
It is associated with Arab nationalist and Arab socialist political ideologies.
It is associated with Mohamed Hassanein Heikal.
It is associated with Willard Range.
It is associated with the FLN in Algeria.
It is associated with Muammar Gaddafi's Libyan Arab Republic.
It is associated with Ba'athist ideology.
It is associated with Saddam Hussein's Ba'athist Iraq.
It is associated with the Assad family's Ba'athist Syria.
ConsistsOf: It consists of Arab socialism, republicanism, secularism, nationalism, anti-imperialism, developing world solidarity, Pan-Arabism, and international non-alignment.
CreatedBy: It was created by the thinking of Gamal Abdel Nasser.
CreatedFor: It was created for liberation towards outside perspectives.
CreatedTo: It was created to give the Arab spirit a new lease on life.
It was created to make Arabs self-confident.
HasA: It has elements of Arab socialism.
It has elements of republicanism.
It has elements of secularism.
It has elements of nationalism.
It has elements of anti-imperialism.
It has elements of developing world solidarity.
It has elements of Pan-Arabism.
It has elements of international non-alignment.
It has a commitment to social reform.
It has a welfare model.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by Arab nationalism.
It is characterized by Arab socialism.
It is characterized by republicanism.
It is characterized by secularism.
It is characterized by anti-imperialism.
It is characterized by developing world solidarity.
It is characterized by Pan-Arabism.
It is characterized by international non-alignment.
It is characterized by pragmatism.
It is characterized by being eclectic, empirical, radical, and yet conservative.
Influenced: It influenced other parts of Africa.
It influenced the Lebanese National Movement.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Gamal Abdel Nasser.
It was influenced by Yugoslav socialism.
IsA: It is an Arab nationalist and Arab socialist political ideology.
It is an attitude of mind.
KnownAs: It is known as at-Tayyār an-Nāṣiriyy.
KnownFor: It is known for combining elements of Arab socialism, republicanism, secularism, nationalism, anti-imperialism, developing world solidarity, Pan-Arabism, and international non-alignment.
It is known for symbolizing the direction of liberation, socialist transformation, the people's control of their own resources, and the democracy of the peoples working forces.
It is known for giving the Arab spirit a new lease on life.
It is known for being a potent political ideology in the Arab world in the 1950s and 1960s.
It is known for being a validation of Nasserism following the Suez Crisis of 1956.
It is known for inspiring national dignity.
It is known for inspiring self-confidence in the Arab community.
It is known for inspiring pride in Egyptians.
It is known for increasing status in Arabs worldwide.
It is known for the mobilization of masses in the political process as participants.
NamedAfter: It is named after Gamal Abdel Nasser.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the Cold War.
It occurred during the Six-Day War of 1967.
It occurred during the Lebanese Civil War.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the Arab world.
It occurred in Egypt.
It occurred in Algeria.
It occurred in Libya.
It occurred in Iraq.
It occurred in Syria.
It occurred in other parts of Africa.
It occurred in the developing world.
It occurred in Lebanon.
It occurred in the Middle East.
UsedAs: It is used as a symbol of Arab political uprising and strength.
It is used as a political ideology.
UsedFor: It is used for political reform.
It is used for socialist transformation.
It is used for the people's control of their own resources.
It is used for the democracy of the peoples working forces.
It is used for modernization of its economy and society.
It is used for spreading revolutionary nationalism in the Arab world.
UsedIn: It is used in the Arab world.
It is used in the wider region.
It is used in Middle Eastern Countries.
UsedTo: It is used to transform Egyptian traditional society.
It is used to attract support in the Arab world.
It is used to transfer the center of decisions concerning the future of the Arab world to the Arab world itself.
